title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 182.420 - Term of directors — removal
text:
182.420.  Term of directors — removal. — Said directors shall hold office, one-third for one year, one-third for two years and one-third for three years, from the first of June following their appointment, and at their first regular meeting shall cast lots for their respective terms; and annually thereafter the mayor shall, before the first of June of each year, appoint, as before, three directors, who shall hold office for three years, and until their successors are appointed.  The mayor may, by and with the consent of the city council, remove any director for misconduct or neglect of duty.
(RSMo 1939 § 14778) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 13474; 1919 § 7207; 1909 § 8203
